identity.name: Help-Desk MFA Reset via Voice Impersonation (Muddled Libra Pattern)
identity.summary: An attacker impersonates a legitimate employee on a voice call to the IT help desk, citing a 'lost phone' or 'failed MFA enrollment,' and pressures the agent into resetting the MFA method and/or password. The attacker then enrolls their own device, completing account takeover without ever touching the endpoint.
identity.description: This is the signature initial-access pattern attributed to Muddled Libra / Scattered Spider / UNC3944 throughout 2024–2026. The attacker pre-stages a target dossier (LinkedIn, breach data, third-party HR leaks), calls the service desk with convincing context (employee ID, manager name, recent project), and exploits agent workflow gaps — typically the lack of out-of-band employee verification. Once MFA is reset, the attacker authenticates to the IdP (Okta, Entra ID), pivots into VPN/SaaS, and proceeds to lateral movement and data exfiltration within hours. Per the 2025 Unit 42 Global IR Report, 65% of initial access in 2024–2025 was identity-driven; ~50% of Muddled Libra cases ended in DragonForce ransomware.

prerequisites.description: Lab Okta tenant with a designated 'victim' user and a help-desk admin role available for simulation.
prerequisites.remediation: Provision a lab Okta tenant; do not run against production.

prerequisites.description: Pre-staged 'attacker' device (e.g., burner laptop or VM) capable of enrolling new factors.

payload.code:
# Simulation: an offline tabletop + scripted API calls reproduce the post-vishing state.
# Step 1 — Help-desk agent (lab role) resets the victim's MFA enrollment via Okta admin API.
curl -sS -X POST "https://${OKTA_TENANT}.okta.com/api/v1/users/${VICTIM_ID}/lifecycle/reset_factors" \
  -H "Authorization: SSWS ${OKTA_HELPDESK_TOKEN}" \
  -H "Accept: application/json"

# Step 2 — Attacker enrolls their own factor (TOTP).
curl -sS -X POST "https://${OKTA_TENANT}.okta.com/api/v1/users/${VICTIM_ID}/factors" \
  -H "Authorization: SSWS ${OKTA_HELPDESK_TOKEN}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"factorType":"token:software:totp","provider":"OKTA"}'

# Step 3 — Attacker authenticates from a new geo + new device fingerprint.
curl -sS -X POST "https://${OKTA_TENANT}.okta.com/api/v1/authn" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"${VICTIM_USERNAME}","password":"${RESET_PASSWORD}"}'

input_variables.name: VICTIM_ID
input_variables.description: Okta user ID of the simulated victim.

expected_artifacts.description: New factor enrollment immediately following reset.
Successful auth from a previously unseen device fingerprint / geo.
Anomalous follow-on activity (admin console access, app assignments).

cleanup.code:
# Revert: remove attacker factor, force user password reset, sign out all sessions.
curl -sS -X DELETE "https://${OKTA_TENANT}.okta.com/api/v1/users/${VICTIM_ID}/factors/${ATTACKER_FACTOR_ID}" -H "Authorization: SSWS ${OKTA_ADMIN_TOKEN}"
curl -sS -X POST   "https://${OKTA_TENANT}.okta.com/api/v1/users/${VICTIM_ID}/lifecycle/expire_password?tempPassword=false" -H "Authorization: SSWS ${OKTA_ADMIN_TOKEN}"
curl -sS -X POST   "https://${OKTA_TENANT}.okta.com/api/v1/users/${VICTIM_ID}/sessions?oauthTokens=true" -H "Authorization: SSWS ${OKTA_ADMIN_TOKEN}"

biocs.name: Okta MFA Reset Followed by Factor Enrollment From New Device
biocs.description: Help-desk-initiated MFA reset followed within 10 minutes by a new factor enrollment AND a successful sign-in from a device fingerprint not previously seen for this user.
biocs.logic:
dataset = saas_okta_raw
| filter event_type in ("user.account.reset_factors", "user.mfa.factor.activate", "user.session.start")
| join (
    user_id, sequence within 10m:
      reset = event_type = "user.account.reset_factors"
      then enroll = event_type = "user.mfa.factor.activate"
      then signin = event_type = "user.session.start" and device_fingerprint not in last_30d_fingerprints[user_id]
  )

biocs.name: Help-Desk Admin Modifies Authentication for User Outside Their Span
biocs.description: Help-desk role performs a factor reset for a user whose department or geo is outside the help-desk admin's usual support population.
biocs.logic:
dataset = saas_okta_raw
| filter event_type = "user.account.reset_factors"
| enrich actor_geo = lookup(actor_user_id, employee_directory.geo)
| enrich target_geo = lookup(target_user_id, employee_directory.geo)
| where actor_geo != target_geo or target_user.privileged = true

xql_queries.name: Confirm reset → enroll → sign-in chain fired in last 30 min
xql_queries.query:
dataset = saas_okta_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type in ("user.account.reset_factors", "user.mfa.factor.activate", "user.session.start")
| fields _time, event_type, actor.alternateId, target[0].alternateId, client.userAgent, client.geographicalContext.country
| sort _time desc

xql_queries.name: Help-desk MFA resets followed by privileged action within 1h (Muddled Libra escalation hunt)
xql_queries.query:
dataset = saas_okta_raw
| filter event_type = "user.account.reset_factors"
| join (dataset = saas_okta_raw | filter event_type = "group.user_membership.add" and target[0].displayName contains "Admin")
  on target_user_id within 1h

correlation_rules.name: Identity Threat — Help-Desk-Assisted Account Takeover
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(Okta MFA Reset Followed by Factor Enrollment From New Device) AND BIOC(Anomalous Sign-In Risk High within 1h)

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Identity Threat Module — anomalous sign-in

products.evidence_query: Playbook: Identity Takeover Response — disable user, kill sessions, force password reset, notify SOC.

use_cases.name: Identity-Driven Initial Access Defense
use_cases.description: Detect and respond to help-desk social-engineering attempts that result in MFA bypass and account takeover.

test_cases.objective: XSIAM Identity Threat Module raises a high-severity incident within 5 minutes of the reset→enroll→sign-in chain.
preconditions: Okta cloud connector configured and ingesting events.
Identity Threat Module enabled with default policy.
steps: Run TTP-2026-0001 payload Step 1 (help-desk-initiated MFA reset).
Run TTP-2026-0001 payload Step 2 (attacker factor enrollment).
Run TTP-2026-0001 payload Step 3 (sign-in from new device fingerprint).
Wait up to 5 minutes; observe XSIAM Incidents view.
success_criteria: Incident created with name matching `Identity Threat — Help-Desk-Assisted Account Takeover`.
Incident timeline shows all three Okta events grouped under one alert chain.

test_cases.objective: XSOAR automatically initiates Identity Takeover Response playbook.
success_criteria: User sessions terminated via Okta API within 2 minutes of incident creation.
SOC notification posted to designated channel.

test_cases.objective: Prisma Access SAML anomaly signal correlates with XSIAM incident.
success_criteria: Prisma Access logs show signal `SAML-Auth-AnomalousDeviceGeo` for the same principal within the incident window.
Correlation visible in XSIAM incident War Room.

preventive_controls: Out-of-band identity verification at help desk (manager callback, video confirmation, or hardware-bound challenge).
FIDO2 phishing-resistant MFA — disallow SMS/voice/TOTP for privileged roles.
Conditional access requiring compliant managed device for sensitive apps.
Help-desk role hardened: no MFA reset without ticket + secondary approver.
detection_engineering: Monitor reset→enroll→sign-in chains with new device fingerprint as a single correlated event.
Baseline help-desk admin behavior; alert on cross-region or out-of-span resets.
Correlate Okta events with Prisma Access SAML telemetry.

references.title: Threat Group Assessment: Muddled Libra (Updated May 16, 2025)
A Peek Into Muddled Libra's Operational Playbook
2025 Unit 42 Global Incident Response Report: Social Engineering Edition
